generators.custom: _NuFrRa_(n,a)=>D(void 0,null,function*(){let e=n[n.length-1];if(e==="")return[];let d=`curl -s -H "Accept: application/json" "https://api.npms.io/v2/search?size=20&q=${e}"`,m=`curl -s -H "Accept: application/vnd.npm.install-v1+json" https://registry.npmjs.org/${e.slice(0,-1)}`,l=s=>s[s.length-1]==="@"?a(m):a(d),g=e.startsWith("@")?S(e)>1:e.includes("@");try{let s=JSON.parse(yield l(e));if(g){let o=Object.entries(s["dist-tags"]||{}).map(([p,j])=>({name:p,description:j}));return o.push(...Object.keys(s.versions).map(p=>({name:p})).reverse()),o}return s.results.map(o=>({name:o.package.name,description:o.package.description}))}catch(s){return console.error({error:s}),[]}})

name: -P
--save-prod
options.description: Package will appear in your `dependencies`. This is the default unless `-D` or `-O` are present

name: -D
--save-dev
options.description: Package will appear in your `devDependencies`

name: -O
--save-optional
options.description: Package will appear in your `optionalDependencies`

options.name: --no-save
options.description: Prevents saving to `dependencies`

name: -E
--save-exact
options.description: Saved dependencies will be configured with an exact version rather than using npm's default semver range operator

name: -B
--save-bundle
options.description: Saved dependencies will also be added to your bundleDependencies list

name: -g
--global
options.description: Operates in 'global' mode, so that packages are installed into the prefix folder instead of the current working directory

options.name: --global-style
options.description: Causes npm to install the package into your local node_modules folder with the same layout it uses with the global node_modules folder

options.name: --legacy-bundling
options.description: Causes npm to install the package such that versions of npm prior to 1.4, such as the one included with node 0.8, can install the package

options.name: --legacy-peer-deps
options.description: Bypass peerDependency auto-installation. Emulate install behavior of NPM v4 through v6

options.name: --strict-peer-deps
options.description: If set to true, and --legacy-peer-deps is not set, then any conflicting peerDependencies will be treated as an install failure

options.name: --no-package-lock
options.description: Ignores package-lock.json files when installing

options.name: --omit
options.description: Dependency types to omit from the installation tree on disk

options.name: --ignore-scripts
options.description: If true, npm does not run scripts specified in package.json files

options.name: --no-audit
options.description: Submit audit reports alongside the current npm command to the default registry and all registries configured for scopes

options.name: --no-bin-links
options.description: Tells npm to not create symlinks (or .cmd shims on Windows) for package executables

options.name: --no-fund
options.description: Hides the message at the end of each npm install acknowledging the number of dependencies looking for funding

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Indicates that you don't want npm to make any changes and that it should only report what it would have done

options.description: Output parseable results from commands that write to standard output

options.name: --ignore-scripts
options.description: If true, npm does not run scripts specified in package.json files

options.name: --script-shell
options.description: The shell to use for scripts run with the npm exec, npm run and npm init <pkg> commands

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Shows a table of all active authentication tokens

options.name: --json
options.description: Show output in json format

name: -p
--parseable
options.description: Output parseable results from commands that write to standard output

options.name: --read-only
options.description: This is used to mark a token as unable to publish when configuring limited access tokens with the npm token create command

options.name: --cidr
options.description: This is a list of CIDR address to be used when configuring limited access tokens with the npm token create command

subcommands.name: revoke
subcommands.description: Immediately removes an authentication token from the registry. You will no longer be able to use it

options.name: --registry
options.description: The base URL of the npm registry

options.name: --otp
options.description: One-time password from a two-factor authenticator

generators.custom:
function(n,a){return D(this,null,function*(){var e,d;if(!n.includes("-g")&&!n.includes("--global")){let m=yield a("cat $(npm prefix)/package.json"),l=JSON.parse(m),g=(e=l.dependencies)!=null?e:{},s=l.devDependencies,o=(d=l.optionalDependencies)!=null?d:{};return Object.assign(g,s,o),Object.keys(g).filter(p=>!n.some(F=>F===p)).map(p=>({name:p,icon:"\u{1F4E6}",description:g[p]?"dependency":o[p]?"optionalDependency":"devDependency"}))}else return(yield a("ls -1 `npm root -g`")).split(`
`).map(l=>({name:l,icon:"\u{1F4E6}",description:"Global dependency"}))})}

name: -S
--save
options.description: Package will be removed from your dependencies

name: -D
--save-dev
options.description: Package will appear in your `devDependencies`

name: -O
--save-optional
options.description: Package will appear in your `optionalDependencies`

options.name: --no-save
options.description: Prevents saving to `dependencies`

options.name: -g
options.description: Uninstall global package

name: -w
--workspace
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of the configured workspaces of the current project

name: -ws
--workspaces
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of all the configured workspaces

subcommands.name: unpublish
subcommands.description: Remove a package from the registry

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Indicates that you don't want npm to make any changes and that it should only report what it would have done

name: -f
--force
options.description: Allow unpublishing all versions of a published package. Removes various protections against unfortunate side effects, common mistakes, unnecessary performance degradation, and malicious input

name: -w
--workspace
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of the configured workspaces of the current project

name: -ws
--workspaces
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of all the configured workspaces

subcommands.name: unstar
subcommands.description: Remove an item from your favorite packages

options.name: --registry
options.description: The base URL of the npm registry

options.name: --otp
options.description: One-time password from a two-factor authenticator

options.name: --no-unicode
options.description: Do not use unicode characters in the tree output

options.name: -g
options.description: Update global package

options.name: --global-style
options.description: Causes npm to install the package into your local node_modules folder with the same layout it uses with the global node_modules folder

options.name: --legacy-bundling
options.description: Causes npm to install the package such that versions of npm prior to 1.4, such as the one included with node 0.8, can install the package

options.name: --strict-peer-deps
options.description: If set to true, and --legacy-peer-deps is not set, then any conflicting peerDependencies will be treated as an install failure

options.name: --no-package-lock
options.description: Ignores package-lock.json files when installing

options.name: --omit
options.description: Dependency types to omit from the installation tree on disk

options.name: --ignore-scripts
options.description: If true, npm does not run scripts specified in package.json files

options.name: --no-audit
options.description: Submit audit reports alongside the current npm command to the default registry and all registries configured for scopes

options.name: --no-bin-links
options.description: Tells npm to not create symlinks (or .cmd shims on Windows) for package executables

options.name: --no-fund
options.description: Hides the message at the end of each npm install acknowledging the number of dependencies looking for funding

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Indicates that you don't want npm to make any changes and that it should only report what it would have done

name: -w
--workspace
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of the configured workspaces of the current project

name: -ws
--workspaces
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of all the configured workspaces

name: -w
--workspace
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of the configured workspaces of the current project

name: -ws
--workspaces
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of all the configured workspaces

options.name: --json
options.description: Show output in json format

options.name: --allow-same-version
options.description: Prevents throwing an error when npm version is used to set the new version to the same value as the current version

options.name: --no-commit-hooks
options.description: Do not run git commit hooks when using the npm version command

options.name: --no-git-tag-version
options.description: Do not tag the commit when using the npm version command

options.name: --preid
options.description: The "prerelease identifier" to use as a prefix for the "prerelease" part of a semver. Like the rc in 1.2.0-rc.8

options.name: --sign-git-tag
options.description: If set to true, then the npm version command will tag the version using -s to add a signature

--workspace
options.description: Enable running a command in the context of the configured workspaces of the current project
